candidates.title: Nearly $139M in drugs seized in southern Ontario police bust involving CBSA
candidates.description: A drug trafficking investigation instigated by the Windsor Police Service has resulted in one of the most significant drug seizures in a single investigation in Ontario, Ontario Provincial Police say.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Mafia fugitive or innocent son? Italy seeks to extradite Ontario man over mob ties
candidates.description: Italy is seeking to extradite Carlo Bruzzese, a Toronto-area man accused of serving as a high-ranking member of the powerful Calabrian 'ndrangheta criminal syndicate, CBC News has learned. The son of a purported mob boss, the 41-year-old Bruzzese was recently arrested by Toronto police.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: How this clinic convinced 21 new doctors to move to London — and yes, they're accepting patients
candidates.description: London-based Thompson Medical Centre, which operates two clinics in London and one in St. Thomas, has managed to attract 21 new doctors to the region since 2025. The director of the clinic says that amounts to room for roughly 40,000 people to find primary care.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada 'will do whatever it takes' to counter Trump's tariff threat, Carney tells premiers
candidates.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney says his government "will do whatever it takes" to defend Canada's interests as the clock ticks down to stave off U.S. President Donald Trump's latest tariff threat.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: These 3 charts show where Trump's new tariffs could have the biggest impact
candidates.description: These three charts break down which sectors are in the crosshairs, which provinces will be the hardest hit and how the tariffs will be felt on both sides of the border.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Lawyers present arguments in trial of Montreal man accused of killing daughter in New York
candidates.description: Lawyers have made their opening arguments in the trial of a man accused of killing his nine-year-old daughter, Melina Frattolin, in 2025 during a trip to New York.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Cape Breton mayor's endorsement of local motorcycle club criticized by crime expert
candidates.description: Mayor Cecil Clarke says he didn't know the Celtic Crew Motorcycle Club was associated with the Hells Angels when he wrote a letter endorsing their community efforts in the Cape Breton Regional Municipality.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Regina hospitals still haven't cleaned up the hand-washing problem diagnosed in 2018
candidates.description: Saskatchewan Health Authority says hand hygiene is important for preventing hospital-acquired infections, but does not say why auditor's 2018 recommendations have yet to be fully implemented.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada is rolling out new graphic image warnings on cigarette packs
candidates.description: Experts have welcomed a new series of graphic images on cigarette packaging in Canada, which will be required by August 1, noting they lead to 'significant increases in the effectiveness' of health warnings.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Quebec Liberals drop candidate who compared Parti Québécois to Nazis
candidates.description: Actor Michel Savard, slated to run in the Montreal electoral district of Gouin in the next election, has apologized for the Facebook comments, which he made before he became a Liberal candidate.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Swiss man who assaulted protester in Ottawa gets absolute discharge
candidates.description: A Swiss man who pleaded guilty to punching a protester in the face outside an Ottawa defence conference in 2024 was given an absolute discharge Wednesday before exiting the courtroom to angry shouts from supporters of the victim.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ford wants premiers, Carney to be 'more vocal' against Trump, says he 'can't do it alone'
candidates.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford says he would like to see his fellow premiers and Prime Minister Mark Carney be "a little more vocal" in the face of U.S. President Donald Trump's latest tariff threat.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Commonwealth Games track and field preview: Men's relay team, hammer throw champs headline Canada's contenders
candidates.description: CBC Sports' daily newsletter looks at the Canadian track and field stars to watch at the Commonwealth Games in Scotland.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Only 9 Canadian cities can show you The Odyssey as it was filmed. Halifax is one
candidates.description: Christopher Nolan's nearly three-hour epic The Odyssey was shot entirely using 70-mm Imax film — the first film in history to do so.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Why there’s hope Quebec will greenlight direct-to-consumer alcohol sales like other provinces have
candidates.description: Nine premiers across Canada have agreed it’s time to remove the trade barriers preventing people from buying alcohol from other provinces. Christine Fréchette says Quebec subscribes to the objectives of the agreement, but certain laws haven’t been amended that would allow it to come into force.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Haitians in the U.S. brace for deportation as Trump ends their protected status
candidates.description: Haitians' permission to live and work in the U.S. is set to expire in less than a week. In Springfield, Ohio, where Haitians make up roughly one-quarter of the population, there's concern that federal agents could immediately start targeting the community for detention and deportation.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Iran will pay for any Houthi-led attacks, Trump says
candidates.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Thursday he would hold ‌Iran accountable for any attacks by Yemen's Houthi militants, who ​said this week they ​were imposing a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Mexican authorities probe alleged scam that wiped out Canadian couple’s retirement savings
candidates.description: Cindy Thompson and Glenn Brown, who live in Curve Lake, Ont., sent $280,000 Cdn to a company co-owned by a British and Mexican national who promised big returns on a condo-flip play in Playa del Carmen that never materialized.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: EU fines Alphabet $1B US for Google Play, search infractions
candidates.description: Google was fined a total of 890 million euros ($1.01 billion US, or $1.43 billion Cdn) on Thursday for flouting European Union rules aimed at reining in ‌the power of Big Tech, the European Commission said.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S., Saudi Arabia sign nuclear co-operation agreement
candidates.description: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia on Wednesday forged a new agreement that could potentially provide the kingdom with uranium enrichment capability for its civilian nuclear program.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Lebanon's prime minister visits shattered border village as phased Israeli withdrawal begins
candidates.description: Lebanon's prime minister visited Wednesday a battered southern village as the implementation of a Washington-brokered Lebanese-Israeli agreement began, following months of war between Israel and the Iran-backed Hezbollah .
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Houthis adding to pressure on Iran conflict, as U.S. undertakes 12th consecutive night of strikes
candidates.description: The Iranian-aligned Houthis on Thursday claimed military strikes on two Saudi oil tankers as part of a naval blockade of Saudi Arabia, advancing toward a second chokehold on global oil supplies at the Red Sea in co-ordination with Iran's attempt to control the Strait of Hormuz.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: France's heat wave in June tied to 5,700 more deaths than normal
candidates.description: At least 5,700 more people than usual died in France last month during a historic heat wave, French public health officials say.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada urges U.K. to join new global bank for defence projects
candidates.description: The High Commissioner for Canada in the U.K. Bill Blair says the government is engaged in talks urging the U.K. to join its multinational defence bank now that Andy Burnham is the new prime minister.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Independent autopsy of Black Mississippi teen Nolan Wells finds 'undetermined' cause of death
candidates.description: An independent autopsy commissioned by the family of Nolan Xavier Wells found the 18-year-old's cause and manner of death to be "undetermined" pending further investigation.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt’s daughter Vivienne files to drop Pitt's last name
candidates.description: Brad Pitt and Angelina Jolie’s daughter Vivienne wants to legally remove “Pitt” from her last name. She's the latest of their six kids to legally drop the name.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Snoop Dogg's life is being made into a movie, due in theatres next summer
candidates.description: Next summer, Snoop Dogg's storied life will come to the big screen in a new biopic from Universal Pictures. The theatrical release of Snoop is scheduled for Aug. 6, 2027, and Outer Banks star Jonathan Daviss will be playing the hip-hop icon.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: NYC Mayor Mamdani calls Israel's Netanyahu a 'war criminal' but admits he can't act on ICC arrest warrant
candidates.description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani said late Tuesday that the city would not be able to execute an arrest warrant issued by the world's top war-crimes court for Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu, but urged the federal government to do so.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump's new tariffs on Canada could face a court challenge. But this time, he might win
candidates.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is imposing his new tariffs on Canadian exports under a section of law that has never been used since it was put on the books in 1930.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: OpenAI model went rogue, hacked another company's system during testing
candidates.description: OpenAI said on Tuesday that an autonomous agent powered by its advanced artificial intelligence models went rogue during a security test and triggered ‌a hack that compromised the infrastructure of the AI startup Hugging Face last week.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Austria turns Hitler's first home into police station, a move opposed by Holocaust survivors group
candidates.description: The building Adolf Hitler was born in near the Austrian border with Germany officially begins a new life as a police station on Wednesday, a conversion Austria's government ‌hopes will prevent it serving as a place of pilgrimage for neo-Nazis.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: How wildfire smoke travels from Ontario to New York City — and turns distant city skies orange
candidates.description: Wildfires that have torched huge areas of forest and devastated communities are also sending smoke far and wide, prompting air quality warnings in distant cities and heated political rhetoric from the U.S. Experts say there's nothing particularly unusual about this summer's smoke patterns — but they say wildfires are getting worse, and that's not likely to stop on either side of the border.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Kensington Palace marks Prince George's 13th birthday with new photo
candidates.description: Prince George turned 13 on Wednesday, and Kensington Palace released a new photo of the young royal to mark the occasion.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Oil tops $100 US per barrel, as Tesla and Alphabet drag stocks lower
candidates.description: It's the first time oil has risen above that benchmark of $100 since late May. Stocks sank under the pressure of rising oil prices, while companies with big fuel bills fell on worries about higher expenses.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Chicken thighs are how much? Here's what's driving the latest grocery sticker shock
candidates.description: Prices for fresh or frozen chicken thighs jumped 8.2 per cent from May to June — far above the monthly increases for the usual offenders like beef and coffee, according to inflation numbers.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Frustration, worry and hope: Canadian businesses brace for the latest round of U.S. tariffs
candidates.description: While some businesses plan to hold steady in the face of a new U.S. tariff threat, others are dreading what they say could be a death knell. And with specific industries likely to be hit hard, business leaders are hoping the new threats could spur momentum on a trade deal with the U.S.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Sobeys' parent company vows change to practice that blocked competing stores
candidates.description: One of Canada's major grocers has announced a new approach to competition around the land where its stores are located.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Oil prices hit $100 for the first time since May
candidates.description: The price of Brent crude rose more than 6% on Thursday as the war in the Middle East continues to escalate.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
candidates.description: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Yemen's Houthis attack Saudi tanker as US launches more Iran strikes
candidates.description: The Iran-backed group, which previously announced a "maritime embargo" against Saudi Arabia, also claims to have hit another vessel.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
candidates.description: Canada's industry is in the spotlight after the US president singled it out as one of three main irritants used to justify new tariffs.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'Let's enjoy the ride' says Elon Musk, as AI fears mount
candidates.description: Talking to The Economist, Musk said he doesn't care if people hate him and said he was not a racist.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Blocked by censors, China's animal lovers take fight against abuse offline and overseas
candidates.description: The killing of a dog and her puppies in Guangdong has sparked outrage inside and outside the country.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: South Korea woman acquitted of murdering newborn after hospital delivery
candidates.description: The woman, who had wanted to end her pregnancy at 36 weeks, gave birth to a baby who doctors later killed.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
candidates.description: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Wreckage of Pan Am plane that shaped aviation safety found 74 years on
candidates.description: The deaths of 52 people on the Clipper Endeavor led to the introduction of mandatory pre-flight safety briefings.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
candidates.description: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Fire kills 10 members of same family in Peru, police say
candidates.description: Authorities are investigating the cause of the blaze in Lima as local media report it may have been arson.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Mamdani backs off pledge to arrest Netanyahu citing lack of authority
candidates.description: The New York City mayor instead called on US authorities to act on International Criminal Court's arrest warrant against the Israeli prime minister.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Trump's nuclear deal with Saudis jettisons longstanding US demands
candidates.description: Signing a nuclear pact in the Middle East is a bold move that has drawn criticism in the US and in Israel.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'What was their crime?': BBC visits Iran school where strike killed 120 children
candidates.description: Nearly five months on, grief is still raw as a teacher and a mother recall the horror of the attack in Minab.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Indian police cracked down on 'cockroach' protesters. They went home and made memes about it
candidates.description: For 'cockroach' protesters, reels and memes became a way of documenting fear, making sense of violence and refusing to let it have the last word.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: A year after deadly jet crash at Bangladesh school, families demand answers
candidates.description: This week marks one year since a military jet struck a school in Dhaka, killing 36, most of them children.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Liquor store employee narrowly escapes leopard attack
candidates.description: After nearly five hours, officials captured the animal and it was later released back into the wild, local authorities confirmed.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: Beluga whales travel by plane and lorry to US from closed Canadian marine park
candidates.description: Six beluga whales have arrived in Illinois and Texas after being moved from a Canadian marine park that could no longer care for them.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Amazon gaming boss predicts future where players no longer need consoles
candidates.description: Jeff Gattis said the rising cost of gaming tech is likely to mean more people will stream their games online.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
candidates.description: Claims from a 15-year-old boy were set to go to trial next week in Los Angeles, but the case has now been dropped.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Hosepipe ban in force as drought threatens - is your area affected?
candidates.description: More than eight million households have been placed under hosepipe bans - which has raised questions about how effectively water resources are being managed.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: When can you see the Perseid meteor shower?
candidates.description: The Perseid meteor shower is set to light up the night sky, putting on a spectacular treat for skygazers.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: UK's largest onshore wind farm to be rebuilt with fewer, larger turbines
candidates.description: Whitelee Windfarm has been producing electricity for about 20 years on a stretch of moorland near Eaglesham but is reaching the end of its life.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: How farmers could fix Wales' polluted rivers and save money
candidates.description: Farmers have a crucial role in cleaning up one of the UK's most polluted river systems, a study shows.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Tokyo urges men to wear shorts to work, but women say it's 'leg hair harassment'
candidates.description: The Tokyo Metropolitan Government wants workers to ditch the suit and tie for t-shirts, trainers - and shorts.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Butterfly city blooms in shadow of office blocks
candidates.description: A patch of land in Hull city centre has been allowed to grow wild to create a haven for insects.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: In a first, Chinese woman wins the prestigious Fields Medal
candidates.description: Despite criticisms of a brain drain from China to the U.S., the awards come at a time when several high-profile Chinese scholars have returned home, some citing an increasingly hostile academic environment in America.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. signs nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia. And, Nolan Wells' independent autopsy results
candidates.description: The U.S. has signed a nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia. And, the independent autopsy results of Nolan Wells fuel more questions surrounding his cause of death.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia's nuclear deal hinges on the kingdom normalizing Israel ties
candidates.description: After a Saudi nuclear power deal raised alarm in Israel, President Trump announced online that the arrangement is subject to the kingdom normalizing relations with Israel through the Abraham Accords.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. signs commercial nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
candidates.description: The agreement between the U.S. and Saudi Arabia gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Former State Department official on the implications of the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal
candidates.description: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Daniel Benaim, a former State Department official who worked on Gulf issues, about the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Ebola death toll in DR Congo surpasses 1,000
candidates.description: Congo's Ebola outbreak has crossed the grim 1,000-death threshold, marking the fastest fatal surge in recorded history of the virus.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Beloved educator's detention fuels India's biggest protests in years
candidates.description: In India, the detention of a beloved educator on hunger strike has turned into its largest anti-government protests in years.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trump administration signs commercial nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
candidates.description: The agreement gives American companies priority access to nuclear reactors and fuel to Saudi Arabia. It's expected to last decades and be worth billions of dollars.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Why the U.S. and Saudi Arabia signed a nuclear agreement
candidates.description: NPR has confirmed a deal that would allow the U.S. to transfer nuclear technology to Saudi Arabia and allow the country to begin enriching uranium for civilian use.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: What to know about Ukraine's military shakeup
candidates.description: After a week of nationwide protests over the direction of Ukraine's military strategy in the ongoing war with Russia, the Ukrainian military has a new commander, Mykhailo Drapatyi. Here's what to know.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: 'New York Times' alleges White House abuses legal system to intimidate reporters
candidates.description: The New York Times will argue in court Thursday that the White House is abusing the justice system to intimidate reporters over a story that angered President Trump.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: A town renamed its festival 'AI Love Irondale Day.' Then came the comments
candidates.description: The online outcry over the renaming of a summer festival illustrates the unease over the data center boom in the South.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: In South Dakota, public media endures a year after federal funding was wiped out
candidates.description: We check in on one state public broadcasting network a year after President Trump signed a law ending federal funding of public media.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Staying in a job for the health insurance? About 1 in 4 Americans do, a survey says
candidates.description: About 24% of U.S. employees stay in unwanted jobs just for health insurance, a report out Wednesday finds. Up significantly since 2021, the uptick in "job lock" is a concerning sign for the U.S. economy.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Get into med school in the graphic memoir 'See One, Do One, Teach One'
candidates.description: Grace Farris' book brings readers behind the med school scenes — documenting anxiety over loans, bouts of impostor syndrome, friendships forged in stress, and the ever-impossible work-life balance.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: This AI tool promises a 'second pair of eyes' to clinicians. Did patients benefit?
candidates.description: Medical workers in a Nairobi clinic used an AI tool to check their work. A new study evaluates the result.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Why health officials do not have a good handle on the current Ebola outbreak
candidates.description: To get slightly ahead of the virus, health officials need to quickly identify people at risk of infection, a practice known as contact tracing. That's a challenge in the Democratic Republic of Congo.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Number of cyclosporiasis cases in the U.S. has surpassed previous records, CDC says
candidates.description: It can take weeks to confirm a single case of intestinal illness caused by the cyclospora parasite, but the CDC's tally for this year now stands at over 4,100.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: FDA panel considers easing peptide restrictions
candidates.description: Advisers to the Food and Drug Administration are weighing the evidence for seven different peptides. Favorable votes would pave the way for easier access.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: African countries are facing rising rates of cancer. What's being done about it?
candidates.description: Cancer diagnoses are on the rise in sub-Saharan Africa and the Eastern Mediterranean regions, but care lags behind.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: A strange transmissible cancer is spreading through the catfish in this lake
candidates.description: For more than a decade, there's been something fishy going on in a lake that straddles Vermont and the province of Quebec. It involves cancer, a kind of catfish, and — possibly — clues about how tumors metastasize.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: In pursuit of 'Instagram face,' are we losing the imperfections that make us human?
candidates.description: Plastic surgery is becoming so normalized and undetectable, it's changing our relationship to reality. The New Yorker staff writer Jia Tolentino considers how beauty standards have dovetailed with AI.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Peptides get their regulatory close-up
candidates.description: A panel of advisers to the Food and Drug Administration will meet this week to make recommendations on whether specialized pharmacies should be allowed to make some popular wellness peptides.
candidates.author: NPR

Minor hail damage to vegetation is possible. Locations impacted include... Trinchera.

candidates.title: M 5.0 - 38 km SSE of Spearman, Texas
candidates.description: The United States Geological Survey reported an earthquake of magnitude 5. The event occurred near 38 km SSE of Spearman, Texas. Official monitoring data may be updated as scientists review additional measurements.
candidates.author: U.S. Geological Survey

candidates.title: LKValues: Aligning Large Language Models with Sri Lankan Societal Values
candidates.description: Value alignment of Large Language Models (LLMs) has been shown to be culturally biased toward Western norms. This results in the mishandling of local values in multilingual societies such as Sri Lanka that have their unique cultural dynamics. Existing benchmarks overlook Sri Lankan-contextualized values in its official language Sinhala, hindering culturally sensitive evaluation and fine-tuning. To bridge this gap, we propose LKValues, the first survey-grounded resource suite for Sri Lankan value alignment. From a trilingual survey of 205 respondents, blending adapted global frameworks and LLM-elicited local constructs, we derive 40 majority-endorsed societal values. Using these values, we construct LKvaluesIT, a Sinhala-English news-derived instruction corpus containing 150k scenario-based instances, and LKvaluesBench, a value-sensitive evaluation benchmark of 1,000 instances. We evaluate a set of proprietary and open-weight LLMs with LKvaluesBench. We fine-tune three open-weight base models (Qwen3.5-4B-Base, Qwen3.5-9B-Base, and Aya-Expanse-8B-Base). Our experiments show that newer and larger LLMs still exhibit low-resource and cultural value-alignment gaps. LKValues fine-tuning improves Qwen-family models in English and Sinhala, reducing invalid outputs and cross-lingual disparities, though gains remain model-family dependent. These highlight LKValues efficacy in embedding Sri Lankan values, offering a replicable pipeline for low-resource, country-specific pluralist value alignment. The dataset is publicly available at https://github.com/NextME14/LKValues.
candidates.author: Nethmi Muthugala, Supryadi, Surangika Ranathunga, Nisansa de Silva, Ruijie Tao, Ovindu Gunatunga, Pengyun Zhu, Shaowei Zhang, Jingting Zheng, Deyi Xiong

candidates.title: SoftReason: A Fully Differentiable Neuro-Soft-Symbolic Deductive Reasoning Architecture over High-Dimensional Perceptual Data
candidates.description: In many reasoning problems, the premises are not observed as discrete symbols, but must be inferred from high-dimensional inputs. Further, the predicate vocabulary, argument structure, and trusted evidence are supplied by a Knowledge Graph (KG), or rule definitions. Classical neuro-symbolic pipelines have a discrete interface between perception and deduction. We present a neuro-soft-symbolic architecture for differentiable deductive reasoning over latent perceptual facts and knowledge-provided predicates. SoftReason removes the gradient gap by representing the deductive state as a local soft interpretation tensor over candidate constants and predicates. Perception proposes probabilistic base facts, KG triples enter as high-confidence soft evidence, and every query anchor, predicate choice, and closure update remains differentiable. Our core innovation is a learned differentiable lift of the immediate-consequence operator. It uses predicate-definition embeddings and latent composition channels to form soft body-predicate mixtures, aggregate over all possible witnesses, propose query-conditioned head facts, and update the interpretation through a monotone probabilistic OR. We instantiate the framework on Knowledge-aware Visual Question Answering (KVQA), and demonstrates how SoftReason supports end-to-end perceptual grounding, KG evidence injection, and differentiable deductive closure in one trainable architecture.
candidates.author: Wael AbdAlmageed

candidates.title: Towards Miniature Humanoid Tele-Loco-Manipulation Using Virtual Reality and Reinforcement Learning
candidates.description: Full-sized humanoid robot capabilities have grown exponentially in recent years, aiming towards general-purpose deployment in human environments. A popular control method used by manufacturers utilizes Virtual Reality for upper-body teleoperation and Reinforcement Learning for lower-body balance and locomotion control. As a result, a single remote operator can see, manipulate, and navigate about a real, distant physical environment. This powerful control stack is often relegated to expensive full-sized robots, many of which are inaccessible to the research community. Miniature humanoids are more prevalent, but employ less biomimicry in their design (e.g. fewer sensors, Degrees of Freedom, etc) and lack similar developments. This paper describes a compliant full-body telepresence control stack developed from the ground up for miniature humanoids. Framework experimentation on ROBOTIS OP3 hardware showcases walking at speeds up to 0.45 m/s independent of arm motions. Tele-loco-manipulation is demonstrated via a cube relocation experiment with an expert human operator. On average, the teleoperated system moved 2 different 40 g cubes within 10 mins, walking a total distance of 5 m. Overall, the developed system shows potential for miniature humanoid tele-loco-manipulation.
candidates.author: Nicolas Kosanovic, Jordan Dowdy, Jean Chagas Vaz

candidates.title: Distributed Acoustic Localization Array Deployed Using a Soft Everting Vine Robot
candidates.description: Soft robot exteroception is increasingly being explored for a variety of field applications. In this work, we present a sound-based system for localizing disaster victims in confined and unstructured environments, based on a distributed acoustic sensing architecture embedded along the body of a soft everting vine robot. We propose a dynamic Steered Response Power with Phase Transform framework that supports both far-field direction-of-arrival estimation and near-field three-dimensional source localization as the robot approaches the sound source. To better understand the design and control space related to localizing sound using a soft, shape-morphing robot body, we conduct experiments measuring the accuracy of these methods for a five-microphone array attached to the robot body using three placements relative to the outer membrane of the robot (inside the pressurized body, inside the inner tail, and outside the outer wall) and in four robot configurations (linear, double linear, circular, and sinusoidal). We measure the change in accuracy as the signal-to-noise ratio, the direction of approach, and the distance of the sound source from the center of the array change. Finally, we demonstrate a vine robot growing into an arbitrary shape while carrying microphones along its outer wall, and show that a sound source located with the array's near field can be localized with high accuracy after only three microphones have everted from the robot body. These results highlight the potential of distributed acoustic sensing for reliable victim localization using soft growing robots.
candidates.author: Sebastian Lorca Godoy, Ciera McFarland, Michael Val, Antonio Alvarez Valdivia, Nathaniel Hanson, Margaret McGuinness

candidates.title: Persian Pixel: A large-scale synthetic OCR dataset for Persian language
candidates.description: Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for Persian remains substantially less mature than for Latin-script languages despite Persian being spoken by more than 110 million people across multiple countries. This gap arises from two fundamental challenges: the intrinsic complexity of the Perso-Arabic writing system and the limited availability of large-scale, high-quality annotated datasets. Persian script exhibits obligatory cursive connectivity, context-dependent glyph shaping, extensive ligatures, diacritic placement, and stylistic variation across writing forms such as Naskh and Nastaliq, all of which significantly complicate text recognition. At the same time, the high cost and labor-intensive nature of manual annotation have created a persistent data bottleneck, limiting the development of robust OCR systems and slowing progress in Persian document digitization.In this paper, we introduce Persian Pixel, a comprehensive synthetic OCR dataset specifically designed to address these challenges. Comprising over 343,000 high-fidelity image text pairs, the dataset spans sentence, paragraph, and full-page document layouts generated from a carefully curated seven-million-word Persian corpus using the SynthOCR-Gen rendering framework. The generation pipeline faithfully models the typographic characteristics of Persian script, including contextual character joining, positional glyph variants, diacritic placement, and multiple representative Persian typefaces. To bridge the synthetic-to-real domain gap, the rendered images are further enriched with more than twenty-five stochastic degradation models that emulate realistic document acquisition artifacts, including ink bleed, paper aging, blur, illumination variation, scanner imperfections, compression artifacts, and multiple noise processes.By overcoming the long-standing scarcity of annotated Persian OCR data, Persian Pixel provides a scalable and openly available resource for training and fine-tuning modern OCR architectures, including transformer-based models such as TrOCR and Donut. The dataset establishes a strong foundation for research in Persian document analysis, historical manuscript digitization, and end-to-end document understanding, while demonstrating that programmatic synthetic data generation offers a practical, cost-effective, and scalable alternative to manual annotation for advancing OCR in low-resource and typographically complex scripts.
candidates.author: Pouria Mahdi, Haq Nawaz Malik

candidates.title: FMRP-LEAN: A HIPAA-Compliant AI-Augmented LIMS Architecture for End-to-End Clinical Assay Workflow Optimization
candidates.description: Clinical biomarker workflows in translational research settings often rely on spreadsheet-driven tracking, manual quality control (QC) reconciliation, and loosely integrated systems, resulting in limited state visibility, delayed reporting, and increased operational risk. These challenges are particularly pronounced in multi-day assays such as Luminex-based quantification of Fragile X Messenger Ribonucleoprotein (FMRP), where HIPAA-compliant data governance, deterministic workflow progression, and coordinated communication across laboratory and clinical teams are required. This paper presents FMRP-LEAN, a HIPAA-compliant, AI-augmented Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) architecture that formalizes biospecimen lifecycle management through a finite-state workflow model with explicit transition guards and dwell-time observability. The system integrates a self-hosted Supabase/PostgreSQL stack deployed within hospital-controlled infrastructure, hybrid edge-internal isolation with encrypted tunneling and loopback-only services, and bi-directional REDCap synchronization. A unified MRN-UUIDv7 identifier framework with QR-based tracking ensures traceable clinical-research linkage under PHI residency constraints. FMRP-LEAN incorporates automated statistical QC pre-screening and a governance-constrained AI operations module that operates exclusively on aggregate projections, with deterministic fallback guarantees. Deployment demonstrates improved workflow observability, reduced QC latency, and enhanced cross-role transparency between laboratory technicians, research coordinators, and patient-facing teams. The architecture provides a reproducible model for secure, state-explicit, and AI-augmented clinical research workflows in regulated healthcare environments.
candidates.author: Eva McCord, Ernest Pedapati, Zag ElSayed

candidates.title: Train the Model, Not the Reader: Decodability Supervision for Verifiable Activation Explanations
candidates.description: Natural-language autoencoders score explanations of hidden activations by reconstruction: an explanation is deemed faithful if the activation can be regenerated from it. The test is structurally insensitive to individual false claims: if flipping a claim does not change the reconstruction, the claim is never penalized. We show the test is passed in two ways, neither faithful. On a released Qwen-2.5-7B verbalizer, explanations reconstruct well above chance while ~2% of specific claims are reconstruction-dependent, so the score tracks gist, not specific facts. Under exact synthetic ground truth, the standard recipe develops co-adapted private codes (false wording the reconstruction depends on) in 5/5 runs, and fixes that leave the target model unchanged do not help. We contribute two audit protocols, the grounded-vs-true cross and the evaluator swap, and RECAP (Readable Encodings via Co-trained Auxiliary Predictors): linear heads trained alongside the target model to keep designated content decodable. On RECAP-trained sandbox models, fresh verbalizers state the designated content truly and the codes vanish, at a +0.001-nat cost. This replicates on a pretrained Pythia-160M: the content becomes reliably probe-decodable, though a fresh verbalizer conveys it only in part (truth 0.44-0.46 vs a near-zero control). For interpretability, high reconstruction does not certify individual claims. For AI safety, RECAP makes designated internal content independently checkable against probes rather than asserted by prose a model can game: an independent probe scores the verbalizer's true claims above its false ones (AUC 0.96, vs 0.82 without RECAP). Against an adversary that edits an explanation to maximize the reconstruction score while lying (suppressing ~87% of its lie penalty), the RECAP probe still flags the lies (AUC 0.95) while the control probe collapses to chance (0.51).
candidates.author: Hiskias Dingeto

candidates.title: Notes to Self: Can LLMs Benefit from Experiential Abstractions?
candidates.description: Humans distill experience into reusable abstractions, e.g., strategies and cautionary reminders, and apply them to gradually solve problems more effectively. We study whether Large Language Models (LLMs) can similarly benefit from such experiential abstractions. From LLMs' solution traces on the MATH training set, a stronger teacher or the LLMs themselves extract natural-language abstractions into a retrievable library. We explore two usage modes: (1) inference-time retrieval and (2) reinforcement learning (RL) with abstraction-augmented training prompts. Experiential abstractions improve LLM performance on mathematical and logical reasoning benchmarks. Self-extracted abstractions match teacher-extracted ones, and our abstraction usage framework can transfer to other datasets and models. These findings suggest LLMs can extract and apply experiential abstractions much as humans leverage distilled experience.
candidates.author: Chang Liu, Xinyu Li, Artur Dubrawski

candidates.title: Distributed Motion Planning with Safety Guarantees for Self-Reconfiguring Robotic Boats
candidates.description: Aquatic self-reconfigurable robots must assemble into desired shapes while ensuring safe interactions among multiple agents. This paper proposes a hybrid framework that combines distributed Model Predictive Control (MPC) with Control Barrier Functions (CBFs) for multi-agent shape formation and reconfiguration. Given a desired shape and target assignment, a distributed MPC scheme, solved via the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM), computes coordinated trajectories through local optimization and information exchange. To ensure safety in real time, distributed CBF-based filters are applied to enforce inter-agent collision avoidance. The proposed approach leverages the predictive capabilities of MPC to mitigate local minima, while CBFs provide formal safety guarantees despite the nonconvexity of the underlying optimization problem. Simulation results with up to 25 agents and experimental validation with four physical robots demonstrate the effectiveness and scalability of the framework.
candidates.author: Alejandro Gonzalez-Garcia, Wei Wang, Wei Xiao, Wilm Decre, Jan Swevers, Carlo Ratti, Daniela Rus

candidates.title: Test-Time Training for Modality Order Consistency in Vision-Language Models
candidates.description: We find that vision-language models are sensitive to a specific semantically irrelevant change: the order in which the image and question are presented. Across three models and three benchmarks, image first prompting consistently outperforms question-first prompting, revealing a repeatable modality order failure. We use this gap to design an order-consistent test-time training method. Our method substantially closes the modality-order gap across all evaluated settings. Surprisingly, it also yields consistent improvements in the stronger image-first branch over the baseline, hence bootstrapping both orderings toward mutual consistency. Activation patching localizes the ordering failure to a narrow mid-network region where representations diverge sharply between prompt orders. We find that the test-time training method repairs this misalignment across layers. Together, our results identify modality-order sensitivity as a circuit-level failure in VLMs and demonstrate that simple, asymmetric test-time adaptation can effectively mitigate it and even improve performance over the baseline.
candidates.author: Aditi Gupta, Yossi Gandelsman

candidates.title: Generative AI floods and dilutes the market for books
candidates.description: Generative AI can produce book-length works of fiction at near-zero cost. These books are often dismissed as low-quality ``slop'' that buyers will ignore, and are assumed to carry little commercial weight. We test that assumption with full-text AI detection across 14,419 self-published genre-fiction books sold on Amazon from 2023 to 2026, matched to daily sales records through June 2026. None of these books disclose whether or not they contain AI-produced content. We find that books for which we detected substantial AI text ($>$ 25\%) make up a large share of the catalog but a smaller share of sales. Even so, they reach commercial scale, winning a growing share of sales over time and taking more of the scarce top-rank positions once held by books with no detected AI text. Over this period, the number of books with observed sales in a quarter grew 19.2-fold, while quarterly revenue grew only 8.9-fold. The market therefore added selling books faster than it added revenue, and revenue per selling book fell across most genres. Books with no AI text lose the most ground in genres with high AI diffusion, and most of all where Kindle Unlimited availability is high. Among top-selling books, those with substantial AI text draw on more distinctive language from existing books than do books with no AI text; for these books overlap rises with revenue, a gradient we do not detect for books with no AI text. Generative AI can thus reshape a creative market through scale rather than quality. Our results bear directly on the market-effect question at the center of the fair use defense to copyright infringement.
candidates.author: Tuhin Chakrabarty, Xinyue Liu, Jane C. Ginsburg, Paramveer Dhillon

candidates.title: Closing the Lab-to-Store Gap: A Data-Efficient Post-Training and Experience-Driven Learning VLA Framework for Retail Humanoids
candidates.description: Closing the gap between benchmark performance and reliable real-world operation remains a central challenge for Vision-Language-Action (VLA) humanoid robots, which must handle execution errors, distribution shifts, and environmental variability. This paper presents DEED (Data-Efficient Post-Training and Experience-Driven Learning), a systems-level approach evaluated on a supermarket chip-restocking task using a Unitree G1-Edu humanoid robot and the GR00T N1.6 foundation model. DEED comprises three key components: (1) a data-efficient post-training pipeline with control-frequency alignment, data curation, task-relevant visual highlighting, and reduced VLA dependence; (2) a real-world study of experience-driven refinement, adapted from RECAP via a text-based advantage prefix and a vision-language value function; and (3) a latent-space analysis tool for studying in- and out-of-distribution behavior. Our results suggest that bridging the lab-to-store gap is primarily a systems integration challenge rather than an architectural one: careful data design and targeted post-training can transform a policy that fails under naive fine-tuning into a competent real-world system using only a single GPU.
candidates.author: Roger Sala Sisó, Tiago Silvério, Jakob Sand, Tran Nguyen Le
